objective: Rebuild Sage prompt end to end around a lean manifest-driven Code Mode prompt stack and clean out the legacy prompt architecture.
scope_summary: Rebuilt Sage's prompt system around a dedicated prompt package, unified prompt assembly across foreground and LangGraph resume paths, removed XML-era prompt expectations from tests/docs, and updated changelog/workflow artifacts.
constraints: Hard-breaking prompt reset with no compatibility wrapper for the old XML contract.
Keep runtime_execute_code as the only public execution tool.
Do not change the bridge manifest or VM deployment in this work unit.

changes.summary: Added the new prompt builder, capability snapshot helpers, and prompt types.
Replaced the old monolithic implementation with exports from the new prompt package.
Stopped seeding prompt scaffolding into graph state and kept prompt debug metadata on the outer turn result.
Switched graph-side prompt assembly to the shared prompt prelude builder.
Rewrote continuity assertions around the trusted context frame and untrusted context envelope.
Updated architecture docs to describe the Markdown prompt core plus trusted and untrusted frames.
Updated runtime pipeline docs to describe the new prompt envelope and direct Code Mode execution flow.
Updated memory docs to describe the prompt builder instead of the retired XML contract.
Added operator-facing entries for the prompt hard-cut rebuild.

validations.notes: Validated authored artifacts after the work-item plan/report update.
Workflow audit passed after the prompt hard-cut artifact cleanup.
Vitest passed with 108 files and 684 tests.
Application and test TypeScript projects both typechecked cleanly.
Markdown lint and docs link checks both passed.
Tracked workflow doc links passed after updating architecture docs.
Deep trust gate passed, including test audit, shuffled trust runs, tool audit, and mutation score 100.00.

evidence: Prompt-focused Vitest suites passed after the transcript continuity tests were rewritten around prompt debug messages.
The full repository test suite passed with 108 files and 684 tests.
The deep trust gate passed, including mutation score 100.00.
decisions: Use one manifest-driven capability snapshot source for prompt teaching and runtime introspection parity.
Keep the prompt JSON frames compact instead of pretty-printed to meet the lean prompt budget target.
Keep runtime control tools internal to the runtime and stop teaching them as public prompt capabilities.
risks: The runtime still depends on internal wait/cancel control tools behind the scenes even though the prompt no longer teaches them as part of the public contract.
